1.message: Welcome to your pregnancy journey! Stay hydrated, take your prenatal vitamins, and schedule your first antenatal visit as soon as possible.

2.message: Week 2 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

3.message: Week 3 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

4.message: By week 4, the embryo has implanted in your uterus. You may experience early symptoms like fatigue or mild nausea. Rest well and avoid alcohol and smoking.

5.message: Week 5 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

6.message: Week 6 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

7.message: Week 7 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

8.message: Your baby is about the size of a kidney bean! Major organs are forming. Continue taking folic acid and report any heavy bleeding to your doctor immediately.

9.message: Week 9 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

10.message: Week 10 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

11.message: Week 11 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

12.message: You've reached the end of your first trimester — congratulations! The risk of miscarriage drops significantly after this week.

13.message: Week 13 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

14.message: Week 14 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

15.message: Week 15 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

16.message: Your baby can now make sucking motions and may start to hear sounds. You might feel the first flutters of movement soon.

17.message: Week 17 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

18.message: Week 18 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

19.message: Week 19 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

20.message: Halfway there! This is typically when you'll have your anatomy scan. Keep up your iron intake to prevent anaemia.

21.message: Week 21 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

22.message: Week 22 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

23.message: Week 23 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

24.message: Your baby's lungs are developing rapidly. Now is a good time to start thinking about birth plans and antenatal classes.

25.message: Week 25 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

26.message: Week 26 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

27.message: Week 27 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

28.message: You've entered the third trimester. Watch for signs of preeclampsia such as severe headaches or swelling in your hands and face.

29.message: Week 29 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

30.message: Week 30 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

31.message: Week 31 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

32.message: Your baby is gaining weight quickly and preparing for birth. Practice your breathing exercises.

33.message: Week 33 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

34.message: Week 34 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

35.message: Week 35 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

36.message: Your baby is nearly full-term. Pack your hospital bag and make sure your support person is ready.

37.message: Week 37 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

38.message: Week 38 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

39.message: Week 39 — Please add the health guidance message for this week.

40.message: Your due date has arrived! Contact your healthcare provider if you experience strong contractions, water breaking, or reduced fetal movement.
